NCT01247324.name: OPERA-I
NCT01247324.group: Relapsing MS, ocrelizumab 600 mg IV Q24W vs IFN-beta-1a 44 mcg SC 3x/wk (96 wk)
NCT01247324.snippet: Source: Hauser SL et al. N Engl J Med 2017;376:221-234 (OPERA-I).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 821 adults with relapsing-remitting MS (EDSS 0-5.5, >=2 relapses in prior 2 years or >=1 in prior year) randomized 1:1 double-blind double-dummy to ocrelizumab 600 mg IV every 24 weeks (n=410) or interferon beta-1a 44 mcg SC 3x/week (n=411) for 96 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - Annualized Relapse Rate
evidence.text: Annualized relapse rate over 96 weeks: ocrelizumab 0.16 (127 relapses) vs IFN-beta-1a 0.29 (236 relapses); adjusted rate ratio 0.54 (95% CI 0.40 to 0.72; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - CDP and Gd-enhancing Lesions
evidence.text: Pooled OPERA-I/II 12-week confirmed disability progression: ocrelizumab 41 vs IFN 64; HR 0.60 (95% CI 0.45 to 0.81; P<0.001). Gd-enhancing lesions at week 96: 0.02 vs 0.29 per scan (97% reduction).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by region and baseline EDSS. D2 LOW - double-blind double-dummy (matched IV infusions + SC injections for both arms). D3 LOW - 89% completion. D4 LOW - blinded MRI and EDSS assessors. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 55 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Diagnosis of multiple sclerosis, in accordance with the revised McDonald criteria (2010)~* At least 2 documented clinical attacks within the last 2 years prior to screening or one clinical attack in the years prior to screening (but not within 30 days prior to screening)~* Neurologic stability for greater than or equal to (>=) 30 days prior to both screening and baseline~* Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS) score 0 to 5.5 inclusive~Exclusion Criteria:~* Primary progr...

NCT01412333.name: OPERA-II
NCT01412333.group: Relapsing MS, ocrelizumab 600 mg IV Q24W vs IFN-beta-1a (96 wk)
NCT01412333.snippet: Source: Hauser SL et al. N Engl J Med 2017;376:221-234 (OPERA-II).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 835 adults with RRMS randomized 1:1 double-blind double-dummy to ocrelizumab 600 mg IV Q24W (n=417) or IFN-beta-1a 44 mcg SC 3x/wk (n=418) for 96 weeks. OPERA-II was the replicate pivotal trial.

evidence.label: Primary - Annualized Relapse Rate
evidence.text: Annualized relapse rate over 96 weeks: ocrelizumab 0.16 vs IFN-beta-1a 0.29; adjusted rate ratio 0.53 (95% CI 0.40 to 0.71; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met, replicating OPERA-I.

evidence.label: Secondary - CDP and MRI Activity
evidence.text: Pooled OPERA-I/II 24-week CDP: ocrelizumab 30 vs IFN 50; HR 0.60 (95% CI 0.43-0.84). Mean number of T2 lesions per scan: ocrelizumab 0.32 vs IFN 1.41 (77% reduction).

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization stratified by region, EDSS. D2 LOW - double-blind double-dummy. D3 LOW - 89% completion. D4 LOW - blinded MRI and EDSS assessors. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 55 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Diagnosis of multiple sclerosis, in accordance with the revised McDonald criteria (2010)~* At least 2 documented clinical attacks within the last 2 years prior to screening or one clinical attack in the years prior to screening (but not within 30 days prior to screening)~* Neurologic stability for greater than or equal to (>/=) 30 days prior to both screening and baseline~* Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS) score 0 to 5.5 inclusive~Exclusion Criteria:~* Primary prog...

NCT02792218.name: ASCLEPIOS-I
NCT02792218.group: Relapsing MS, ofatumumab 20 mg SC monthly vs teriflunomide 14 mg PO daily (up to 30 mo)
NCT02792218.snippet: Source: Hauser SL et al. N Engl J Med 2020;383:546-557 (ASCLEPIOS-I).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 946 adults with RRMS (EDSS 0-5.5, >=1 relapse in prior year or >=2 in prior 2 years) randomized 1:1 double-blind double-dummy to ofatumumab 20 mg SC monthly (n=465) or teriflunomide 14 mg PO daily (n=481) for up to 30 months (event-driven).

evidence.label: Primary - Annualized Relapse Rate
evidence.text: Annualized relapse rate: ofatumumab 0.11 vs teriflunomide 0.22; adjusted rate ratio 0.49 (95% CI 0.35 to 0.68; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - CDW and Lesion Activity
evidence.text: Pooled ASCLEPIOS-I/II 3-month confirmed disability worsening: ofatumumab 46 vs teriflunomide 68; HR 0.66 (95% CI 0.50-0.86). Gd-enhancing T1 lesions reduced 97%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IVR randomization stratified by region, baseline EDSS. D2 LOW - double-blind double-dummy. D3 LOW - 86% completion. D4 LOW - blinded MRI + EDSS. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 55 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* 18 to 55 years of age~* Diagnosis of multiple sclerosis (MS)~* Relapsing MS: relapsing-remitting MS (RRMS) or secondary progressive MS (SPMS)~* At least 1 relapse during the previous 1 year or 2 relapses during the previous 2 years or a positive gadolinium-enhancing MRI scan in previous year~* Expanded disability status scale (EDSS) score of 0 to 5.5~Exclusion Criteria:~* Primary progressive MS~* Disease duration of more than 10 years in patients with an EDSS score of 2 or...

NCT02792231.name: ASCLEPIOS-II
NCT02792231.group: Relapsing MS, ofatumumab 20 mg SC monthly vs teriflunomide (up to 30 mo)
NCT02792231.snippet: Source: Hauser SL et al. N Engl J Med 2020;383:546-557 (ASCLEPIOS-II).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 955 adults with RRMS randomized 1:1 double-blind double-dummy to ofatumumab 20 mg SC monthly (n=481) or teriflunomide 14 mg PO daily (n=474) up to 30 months. ASCLEPIOS-II was the replicate pivotal trial.

evidence.label: Primary - Annualized Relapse Rate
evidence.text: Annualized relapse rate: ofatumumab 0.10 vs teriflunomide 0.25; adjusted rate ratio 0.41 (95% CI 0.29 to 0.58; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met, replicating ASCLEPIOS-I.

evidence.label: Secondary - CDW and MRI
evidence.text: 6-month confirmed disability worsening: ofatumumab 26 vs teriflunomide 36; HR 0.71 (95% CI 0.50-1.01). Gd-enhancing T1 lesions reduced similarly.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization stratified by region, EDSS. D2 LOW - double-blind double-dummy. D3 LOW - 86% completion. D4 LOW - blinded central MRI + EDSS adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 55 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Male or female patients aged 18 to 55 years at Screening~* Diagnosis of multiple sclerosis (MS)~* Relapsing MS: relapsing-remitting MS (RRMS) or secondary progressive MS (SPMS) with disease activity~* Documentation of at least: 1 relapse during the previous 1 year OR 2 relapses during the previous 2 years OR a positive gadolinium-enhancing MRI scan during the year prior to randomization~* Disability status at Screening with an Expanded Disability Status Scale (EDSS) score ...

title: RapidMeta Neurology | High-Efficacy DMTs for Relapsing MS v1.0
